member.name: T:Etch.Testing.FlakyTestAttribute
member.summary: Marks a test as flaky so that it is excluded from the main CI gate and routed to the dedicated flaky-watch lane instead.
member.remarks: A flaky test must also carry [Category("Flaky")] so that TUnit's filter pipeline routes it correctly; this attribute adds metadata (quarantine date, tracking issue) consumed by the flake detector.

member.name: P:Etch.Testing.FlakyTestAttribute.QuarantinedSince
member.summary: The date (ISO-8601) when the test was quarantined. Used by the flake detector to enforce the 7-day fix deadline.

member.name: P:Etch.Testing.FlakyTestAttribute.TrackingIssue
member.summary: Optional tracking issue URL or ID for the root-cause fix.

member.name: M:Etch.Testing.MemoryReporters.GpuMemoryReporter.EstimateCacheMemory(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Estimates GPU memory used by the render cache for a given scene size. Returns the approximate bytes allocated for textures, buffers, and pipelines. Returns 0 if GPU is unavailable.

member.name: M:Etch.Testing.SceneCpuRenderer.RasterizeSector(Etch.Raster.Cpu.Framebuffer,Etch.Scene.FillSectorPayload@,Etch.Geometry.Affine,System.UInt32,Etch.Raster.Cpu.ClipMask)
member.summary: Rasterizes a filled annular sector (pie/donut slice) directly into the framebuffer. The strip pipeline has no sector primitive, so without this FillSector commands render nothing on the CPU fallback (WP-3514). Angle convention matches the GPU sector instance: point = center + r·(cos θ, sin θ) with screen-Y down.

member.name: T:Etch.Testing.SceneCpuRenderer.CpuRenderCache
member.summary: Reusable CPU render context that pre-computes tile/strip/mask data during construction so that is allocation-free. Create once for a given scene and output size, then call repeatedly.

member.name: P:Etch.Testing.SceneRunner.IsSoftwareGpu
member.summary: True when ETCH_SOFTWARE_GPU=1 is set in the environment. GPU rendering paths should skip hardware-only validation checks (e.g. timestamp queries) when this flag is set.
